rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH HIGER VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Evidence In Support Of Her Oposition To Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for June 6, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANTS DR. DAVID HOLST, M.D., DR. RONALD K. LAGRO, M.D., DIGNITIY HEALTH'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Evidence In Support Of Her Oposition To Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Summary Adjudication. Defendant Dignity Health et al.'s motion to strike Plaintiff's evidence is denied as untimely per CCP 1005(b). In any event, the court construes this motion as an evidentiary objection or response to Plaintiff's separate statement opposing summary judgment, specifically Plaintiff's undisputed material facts 1 and 7. The court has considered these responses in ruling on Defendants' companion motion for summary judgment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH HIGER VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for June 6, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANTS DR. DAVID HOLST, M.D., DR. RONALD K. LAGRO, M.D., DIGNITIY HEALTH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendants Dignity Health, Dr. David Holst, and Dr. Ronald Lagro's motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The court grants summary adjudication in favor of Defendants as to COAs 1, 3, and 5, for discrimination, retaliation, and termination in violation of public policy. The court denies summary adjudication as to the remaining claims, and accordingly denies summary judgment. The tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRA STAFFORD ET AL VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Connor J. Stinson, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for June 6, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens. Hearing required. Defendant Uber Technologies Inc.'s motion to dismiss or stay action based on forum non conveniens is granted in part. The court stays this action so that Plaintiffs may pursue their claim in France. The stay is conditioned on Uber Technologies Inc.'s waiver of any personal jurisdiction or statute of limitations defenses to Plaintiffs' proceeding against it there. The tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: RANJEET DOSANJH VS. SAFEWAY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for June 6, 2025, Line 3. PLAINTIFF RANJEET DOSANJH'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiff Ranjeet Dosanjh moves to quash subpoenas issued by defendant Gardaworld to Dosanjh's mental health treatment providers. The court grants the motion in part and denies it in part. The tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for June 6, 2025, Line 6.
